# Manage alias suffixes

You can edit or delete existing Boundary alias suffixes at any time. Editing a suffix doesn't affect aliases that already use the old value — only new aliases pick up the change — while deleting a suffix requires first removing any aliases that depend on it.

## Permissions

@include '/suffixes/permissions.mdx'

For more information about creating roles and configuring them with permissions, refer to [Manage access with roles](/boundary/docs/rbac/manage-roles).

## Edit a suffix value

You can edit a suffix value. When you change a suffix value, existing aliases continue to use the old suffix, while new aliases use the updated suffix. If you update an existing alias, it will use the new suffix going forward.

Complete the following steps to edit a suffix:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Admin Console" group="admin">

Select whether you want to edit a suffix for an org or project scope:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Org">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org scope.
1. Select **Org Settings** in the navigation pane.
1. Click the suffix, and then click **Edit suffix**.
1. In the **Alias suffix** field, type a unique value for the suffix.
1. Click **Save**.
1. Confirm the change.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="Project">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org scope that contains the project.
1. Select the project scope.
1. Select **Project Settings** in the navigation pane.
1. Click the suffix, and then click **Edit suffix**.
1. In the **Alias suffix** field, type a unique value for the suffix.
1. Click **Save**.
1. Confirm the change.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to edit an existing alias suffix:

   ```shell-session
   $ boundary scopes set-alias-suffix \
       -id o_1234567890 \
       -alias-suffix org1
   ```

   The output confirms the update (verify against a live run):

   ```plaintext
   Scope information:
     ID:                    o_1234567890
     Alias Suffix:          org1
   ```

   You must include the following attributes when you edit a suffix:

   @include 'scopes/set-alias-suffix.mdx'

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Delete a suffix

You can delete a suffix if you no longer need it. Before deleting it, make sure no associated aliases are using the suffix.

Boundary prevents you from deleting a suffix if any aliases are using it. Org suffixes may be associated with aliases in projects nested under the org. You must delete any associated aliases before deleting the suffix.

Complete the following steps to delete a suffix:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Admin Console" group="admin">

Select whether you want to delete a suffix from an org scope or a project scope:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Org">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org scope.
1. Select **Org Settings** in the navigation pane.
1. Click the suffix, and then click **Delete suffix**.
1. Click **Delete** to confirm.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="Project">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org scope that contains the project.
1. Select the project scope.
1. Select **Project Settings** in the navigation pane.
1. Click the suffix, and then click **Delete suffix**.
1. Click **Delete** to confirm.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to delete an alias suffix:

   ```shell-session
   $ boundary scopes remove-alias-suffix \
       -id o_1234567890 \
   ```

   The output confirms the removal (verify against a live run):

   ```plaintext
   Scope information:
     ID:                    o_1234567890
     Alias Suffix:          (none)
   ```

   You must provide the following attribute when you delete a suffix:

   @include 'scopes/remove-alias-suffix.mdx'

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## More information

For more information about aliases, refer to [Overview of aliases](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration).

If you haven't created a suffix yet, refer to [Create an alias suffix for a scope](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration/create-suffix).
